title: Violent organised crime syndicates linked to illegal tobacco may be coming to Darwin | ABC NEWS
description: Convenience stores selling cheap illicit tobacco products are popping up across Darwin, fuelling fears of organised crime syndicates setting up shop in the Northern Territory.   Police say they've poured significant resources into preventing major crime gangs from getting a foothold in the NT, but legitimate retailers say they're being forced out of an increasingly competitive market. Olivana Lathouris has this report for ABC News Northern Territory. title: How important is it to keep the Bab el-Mandeb Strait open? | The Business | ABC NEWS
description: Markets may have the jitters but how realistic is it that the Houthis could open a new front in the war? The US has warned the rebel group could threaten shipping near the Bab el-Mandeb Strait between the southern Red Sea to the Gulf of Aden. Such action could impede Saudi Arabia's oil shipments. One of Australia's leading experts on international law, and law of the sea, ANU's professor Donald Rothwell says the strait is critical because it's an "international maritime highway in the sense that it connects the Mediterranean via the Suez Canal, through the Red Sea, and then ultimately out into the Indian Ocean."  He says if that strait is closed, the effective passage of all commercial shipping through the Suez Canal will be blocked. "And of course, the whole reason why the Suez Canal was built originally was to allow for the free passage of vessels between East and West, effectively." While there are questions over the Houthis' capabilities, Donald Rothwell says there is a legal basis for Australia to feel confident in providing military support in the wider Gulf, which includes the Red Sea. He says "Australia did provide some level of military assistance, not in the form of a ship, but certainly provided some level of military assistance to the United States and the United Kingdom when they were initially responding to those Houthi attacks in the Red Sea in early 2024". But he says the legal basis for Australian to join an international coalition in the Red Sea - if needed - would now be stronger. #ABCBusiness   Subscribe: http://ab.co/1svxLVE  ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. title: Concerns health insurers creeping towards US-style managed care | The Business | ABC NEWS
description: Increasing partnerships between health insurers, GP centres and telehealth services has raised concerns about a potential two-tier system in which privately insured patients have more affordable access to GP consultations.  Since the pandemic, health insurers have increasingly pursued a "vertical integration" strategy, which involves investing in or partnering other businesses along the health service supply chain.  Once, insurers mainly had their own dental or optical service providers. Now insurers are increasingly expanding their reach into GP medical centres and telehealth providers. It has raised alarms among medical groups, which are calling for more guardrails.   Health insurer industry body Private Healthcare Australia chief executive Rachel David says the "health funds are not trying to take over the whole health system. What they're trying to do is to plug gaps in the health system that consumers are experiencing."  Medibank, Bupa, NIB and HCF are featured in this story by Alison Branley.  #ABCBusiness Subscribe: http://ab.co/1svxLVE Read more here: https://www.abc.net.au/news/2026-03-23/calls-for-closer-look-as-private-insurers-move-into-gp-clinics/106337010  ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. title: Dezi Freeman shot dead seven months after shootout which killed two police  | ABC NEWS
description: 00:00 Police have shot dead fugitive gunman Dezi Freeman at a property in Victoria's north-east. 00:47 Victoria Police tracked down Freeman to a remote property at Tholagalong where he allegedly raised a firearm at them. 03:21 The ABC's Ben Knight reports from Thologalong where Victoria's Chief Commissioner Mike Bush gave an update on the police operation. 04:56 ABC reporter Ashleigh Aldridge reports from Dezi Freeman's home town of Porepunkah where residents are relieved to have some closure for the town, and also sad for Freeman's family. 06:08 Freeman was a conspiracy theorist and so-called "sovereign citizen" who chose to live on society's fringes, but it was his actions in August 2025 when he shot and killed two police officers which saw him thrust into the spotlight. title: Assaults on paramedics reach record-levels, senior staff warn conditions are untenable | ABC NEWS
description: Paramedics say St Johns Ambulance service in the NT has reached breaking point as demand for the service reaches record highs. Meanwhile on the ground, assaults on paramedics are reaching record-levels and senior staff warn the conditions are increasingly untenable. Joseph Hathaway Wilson has this report for ABC News Northern Territory.
